howItWorks.intro: This child height predictor uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How accurate is this?
faq.answer: The mid-parent height method predicts within 2-3 inches for about 68% of children. Genetics from grandparents, nutrition, and health can cause variation beyond this range.

faq.question: What is the mid-parent formula?
faq.answer: For boys: (father + mother) / 2 + 2.5 inches. For girls: (father + mother) / 2 - 2.5 inches. This accounts for average sex differences in height.

faq.question: What else affects height?
faq.answer: Nutrition (especially protein and calcium), sleep quality, physical activity, chronic illness, and genetic factors beyond parents (grandparents, ethnic background).

faq.question: When do children reach adult height?
faq.answer: Girls typically reach adult height by 14-16, boys by 16-18. A growth spurt during puberty adds the final 3-5 inches.

faq.question: Is the Child Height Predictor free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Child Height Predictor?
sections.body: The Child Height Predictor is a free online tool designed for expecting parents who need quick, accurate calculations in the pregnancy and parenting space. By entering your father's height, mother's height, child sex, you get instant results including predicted adult height, likely range, in feet/inches. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting predicted adult height right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In pregnancy and parenting, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by expecting parents worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to prepare for parenthood with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your father's height and need to find the right predicted adult height. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Father's Height (inches)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your father's height in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Mother's Height (inches)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your mother's height in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Child Sex (1=Boy 2=Girl)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your child sex in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your predicted adult height, likely range, in feet/inches. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your father's height before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.

If you're unsure about your child sex, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
